# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

redis:
  version: "latest"
  services:
    master:
      image: redis:latest
      ports:
        - "6379:6379"
      command: redis-server --slaveof no one --appendonly yes
      environment:
        - MIN_REPLICA_TO_WRITE=0
      volumes:
        - ./data/master:/data

    slave1:
      image: redis:latest
      ports:
        - "6380:6379"
      command: redis-server --slaveof master 6379 --appendonly yes
      environment:
        - MIN_REPLICA_TO_WRITE=0
      volumes:
        - ./data/slave1:/data

    slave2:
      image: redis:latest
      ports:
        - "6381:6379"
      command: redis-server --slaveof master 6379 --appendonly yes
      environment:
        - MIN_REPLICA_TO_WRITE=0
      volumes:
        - ./data/slave2:/data

networks:
  default:
    driver: bridge
